content:
I rewrote the workqueue bindings to work together with the pin-init stuff. When/how should I begin to upstream it? Should I wait for anything else to happen first?
You can find it here:
Commit: https://cs.android.com/android/_/android/kernel/common/+/0863f711df7620180f52a4f788178ee27ec9621a
File: https://cs.android.com/android/_/android/kernel/common/+/0863f711df7620180f52a4f788178ee27ec9621a:rust/kernel/workqueue.rs
If the dependencies are already in rust-next, please feel free to start submitting it.
I think offset_of! is still missing, which it needs.
Yeah, I was looking at why const_refs_to_cell was getting enabled... That explains it.
Please feel free to send offset_of as a previous patch on behalf of @Wedson Almeida Filho to get the ball rolling.

Strictly speaking, we could probably drop the const_refs_to_cell requirement by computing the offset in a function rather than in a constant, but I'm worried whether rustc will optimize the stack space usage away.
1.71 is released after the next kernel cycle, so are not in time for using offset_of, but that is fine, we will remove it later.
Oh, Rust is getting a proper offset_of in std?
Yeah!
https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/106655
That's amazing.

So if you are OK with sending Wedson's patch as a previous patch in the same series, then please just go ahead with offset_of. In the commit message for offset_of, just add a note explaining that offset_of is coming to the standard library soon, so the unstable feature may be dropped soon anyway.

Also, if you make the macro #[macro_export], you will likely want to enable the const_refs_to_cell feature in rust_allowed_features (scripts/Makefile.build). Otherwise, if we can keep it private to kernel, we can avoid that until we need it, which seems best.
Which commit do you want me to base the patches on? I need pin-init to already be there.
rust-next is fine.
Should I also add container_of!? I don't need it.
You mean because 6646d85a8a72 added it together with offset_of, right? No, I think it is best to drop it.

@Alice Ryhl how should I send my feedback? Should I wait until it is on the list? Aside from docs and some refactoring questions I think the usage of pin-init looks good. I am also curious about certain parts of the design (maybe something for the meeting?).

y86 said:

Alice Ryhl how should I send my feedback? Should I wait until it is on the list? Aside from docs and some refactoring questions I think the usage of pin-init looks good. I am also curious about certain parts of the design (maybe something for the meeting?).

I have some feedbacks too, and I plan to wait until Alice sends to the mail list
I will be submitting it soon.
Probably later this week

I am sending v2 soon. Here is the series I intend to submit: <https://github.com/Darksonn/linux/commits/alice/workqueue>
If you want to add something before I submit it, you can do so here.
@Alice Ryhl sorry, I forgot to send my drafts I had already prepared. I just sent them!
I'll check those out and update accordingly
In general, the function stored in the work_struct has to know what the pointer type is. Otherwise it cannot release the workqueue's ownership of the value.
I think I've figured out a way to simplify the workqueue bindings without needing the field projections.
Workqueue v3 was just sent: <https://lore.kernel.org/all/20230711093303.1433770-1-aliceryhl@google.com/>
By the way, I dropped Reviewed-bys from a few of the patches because of the changes. I'd appreciate it if you all could look at those again even if you reviewed them in v2.
sure, I will take a look when I find the time.
